creator: Director: George Cukor / Writers: Ivan Davis, Emlyn Williams (play) / Starring: Katharine Hepburn, Ian Saynor, Bill Fraser
description: A strong-willed teacher, determined to educate the poor and illiterate youth of an impoverished Welsh village, discovers one student whom she believes to have the seeds of genius in him. Top rate film. **The scene in which Katharine Hepburn 's character is riding up a hill on a bicycle was intended to be shot with a stunt double, but Hepburn insisted on doing it herself. After falling off the bicycle several times, however, she overheard a crewmember say, "God, she got old." She approached the man and said, "You're right. I have." Hepburn then signaled for the stunt double to take over, and went back to her hotel. **The original Broadway stage production of "The Corn is Green" starred legendary actress Ethel Barrymore and opened at the National Theatre in New York on 26th November 1940 until it closed on 9th September 1941. It transferred to the Royale Theatre on the latter date and played at the new venue until 17th January 1942. In total at the two theaters, the inaugural Broadway production ran for 477 performances. The stage play has been revived on Broadway at least on three further occasions, with seasons during 3rd May 1943 - 19th Jun 1943 at the Martin Beck Theatre; 11th Jan1950 - 22nd Jan1950 at the City Center; and 22nd Aug 1983 - 18th Sep 1983 at the Lunt-Fontanne Theatre. ** Bette Davis had played Miss Lilly Moffat in the original The Corn Is Green (1945) and was still a working actress when this remake was first broadcast in 1979. **Seventh filmed adaptation of "The Corn is Green" since it was first performed on stage in 1940. The first was a theatrical feature, the rest were made-for-TV films. --IMDb
